impact: Keeps the lifecycle index usable for the next exact-path validator by removing the nonexistent inspection skeleton reference and documenting that the index is based only on committed skeleton fixtures.
summary: Repair lifecycle process index to cite committed lifecycle skeleton fixture paths, including the committed inspection scheduling/result follow-up skeleton path.
target_task: Task checkbox-29: Recovery for checkbox-29: first repair only `ppd/tests/fixtures/lifecycle/permit_lifecycle_process_index.json` so every skeleton fixture path names an already committed lifecycle skeleton fixture. In particular, do not leave a reference to nonexistent `ppd/tests/fixtures/lifecycle/inspection_lifecycle_process_skeleton.json`; use the committed inspection lifecycle skeleton path exactly as it exists on disk. Do not add validator code in this recovery task.

command: files=$(find ppd -name '*.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print); test -z "$files" || npx tsc --noEmit --target ES2020 --module ESNext --moduleResolution node --strict --skipLibCheck --types node $files
